steps.title: Identify the common factor in both terms
steps.content: Looking at $2x^3 + 4$, both terms are divisible by 2. We can write $2x^3 = 2 \cdot x^3$ and $4 = 2 \cdot 2$, so the greatest common factor is 2.

steps.title: Factor out the GCF
steps.content: Factor 2 from both terms: $$2x^3 + 4 = 2(x^3 + 2)$$ This matches choice D: $2 \times (x^3 + 2)$ — D is correct.
